page_content:
KNES289 Topical Investigations (1-6 Credits) Independent study by an individual student or a group of students in special areas of knowledge not covered by regularly scheduled courses. Repeatable to: 6 credits.

KNES293 History of Sport in America (3 Credits) The growth and development of sport in America. The transformation of sport within the perspective of American history, including class sport, professionalization, amateurism, and international involvement.

KNES300 Biomechanics of Human Motion (4 Credits) The study of human movement and the physical and physiological principles upon which it depends. Body mechanics, posture, motor efﬁciency, sports, the performance of a typical individual and the influence of growth and development upon motor performance. Prerequisite: Minimum grade of C- in BSCI201; and minimum grade of C- in MATH113 or higher. Recommended: KNES265 or PHYS121. Restriction: Must be in a major within SPHL-Kinesiology department.
